stages.title: Site Analysis
stages.narration: Stage 1. Site analysis. Geogrid retaining walls are constructed where unstable slopes threaten infrastructure.
stages.explanation: Geogrid retaining walls are constructed where unstable slopes threaten infrastructure.

stages.title: Geotechnical Investigation
stages.narration: Stage 2. Boreholes are drilled and soil profiles are interpreted to define reinforcement requirements.
stages.explanation: Soil investigation determines stability requirements and reinforcement lengths.

stages.title: Excavation
stages.narration: Stage 3. Slope excavation and bench cuts create a stable platform for reinforced soil construction.
stages.explanation: Excavation creates a stable platform for reinforced soil construction.

stages.title: Foundation Preparation
stages.narration: Stage 4. A leveling pad and compacted granular foundation layer are placed.
stages.explanation: The foundation layer provides uniform support for the retaining system.

stages.title: First Wall Course
stages.narration: Stage 5. The first course of segmental facing blocks is installed and aligned.
stages.explanation: The first course controls alignment of the entire retaining structure.

stages.title: First Geogrid Installation
stages.narration: Stage 6. The first geogrid layer is now installed. The reinforcement extends into the retained soil mass and develops tensile resistance against lateral earth pressure.
stages.explanation: Geogrids transfer tensile forces into the reinforced soil mass.

stages.title: Backfill Placement
stages.narration: Stage 7. Free-draining granular backfill is placed behind the facing units.
stages.explanation: Backfill must be free-draining and properly graded.

stages.title: Compaction
stages.narration: Stage 8. Compaction rollers increase soil density and develop reinforced soil strength.
stages.explanation: Compaction is critical for developing reinforced soil strength.

stages.title: Multiple Geogrid Layers
stages.narration: Stage 9. Additional geogrid layers and facing courses build the reinforced soil block.
stages.explanation: Each reinforcement layer increases stability and resistance to earth pressure.

stages.title: Drainage Pipe Installation
stages.narration: Stage 10. Perforated drainage pipes, filter layers, and drainage blankets are installed.
stages.explanation: Drainage prevents hydrostatic pressure buildup behind the wall.

stages.title: Weep Hole System
stages.narration: Stage 11. Weep holes are formed to safely release trapped pore water.
stages.explanation: Weep holes safely release trapped water.

stages.title: Wall Completion
stages.narration: Stage 12. The wall reaches final height with coping and completed facing panels.
stages.explanation: The reinforced soil mass and facing act together as a stable retaining system.

stages.title: Erosion Control
stages.narration: Stage 13. Surface protection and vegetation control long-term slope erosion.

stages.title: Completed Structure
stages.narration: Construction complete. The geogrid reinforced retaining wall protects the road corridor from landslide failure.
stages.explanation: Mechanically stabilized earth system suitable for hillside infrastructure protection.

geogrid.title: Geogrid Layer
geogrid.tensileResistance: Mobilizes tensile capacity in reinforced soil.
geogrid.anchorageMechanism: Friction and bearing against compacted fill.
geogrid.loadTransfer: Transfers lateral thrust to resistant soil mass.

drainage_pipe.title: Drainage Pipe
drainage_pipe.waterRemoval: Collects and conveys seepage to outlets.

facing_block.title: Facing Block
facing_block.loadTransfer: Transfers facing loads to geogrid connections.

reinforced_soil_zone.title: Reinforced Soil Zone
reinforced_soil_zone.compositeBehavior: Soil-geogrid composite acts as coherent mass.
reinforced_soil_zone.stabilityMechanism: Increases factor of safety against sliding.
